title: Mild Hangover-Cure Canadian Rest-Meat Wrap
description: A floral, molten hangover cure dish featuring vanilla and rest-meat, served in a low-calorie-friendly lotus leaf wrap.
instructions: Warm the lotus leaf wraps in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2 minutes on each side.
Slice the rest-meat into thin strips and set aside.
In a small saucepan, combine the vanilla bean paste, apple butter, honey, and cinnamon powder. Heat over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture is smooth and fragrant.
Add the toasted sunflower seeds to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Brush the saucepan mixture onto the warmed lotus leaf wraps, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Divide the sliced rest-meat among the wraps, placing it in the center of each wrap.
Top the rest-meat with a dollop of plain yogurt, a sprinkle of seeds of sunflower sprouts, and a few leaves of fresh mint.
Add diced pear to the center of each wrap, if desired.
Fold the bottom edge of each wrap up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the wrap into a neat cylinder.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional fresh mint leaves if desired.
Reheat the wraps in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2 minutes on each side, if desired.
Serve with additional saucepan mixture on the side, if desired.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Reheat the wraps in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2 minutes on each side, or until warmed through.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional fresh mint leaves if desired.
Serve with additional saucepan mixture on the side, if desired.
